HTTP_PostIntakeMessage.description: Per-message intake. POST this ONE flagged email to LCC's /api/intake?_route=outlook-message. handleOutlookMessage extracts/stages/dedups it and returns the AUTHORITATIVE decision in the JSON `processing_complete` block (outcome + target_folder + final_target_folder + clear_flag) plus a `correlation_id`. Runs directly off the trigger — NOT dependent on the webLink lookup, so a webLink failure can never block intake or the Teams card.

HTTP_GetEmailWebLink.description: Optional, NON-BLOCKING web-link enrichment. Runs in PARALLEL off the trigger (never gates intake or the card). The Teams card falls back to triggerOutputs()?['body/webLink'] if this fails, so its Succeeded/Failed status is intentionally ignored downstream.

HTTP_GetIntakeSummary.description: Fetch the human-readable summary for the card, keyed on the correlation_id returned by HTTP_PostIntakeMessage. Depends on intake succeeding — NOT on the webLink lookup.

Post_card_in_a_chat_or_channel.description: Teams notification. Runs after HTTP_GetEmailWebLink completes so it can prefer the enriched link, but treats it as OPTIONAL (Succeeded OR Failed) — a webLink failure must never suppress the card. Also requires HTTP_GetIntakeSummary Succeeded for the summary/outcome.

Condition_ShouldMove.description: Guard the move relay: only fire HTTP_ProcessingComplete when the recorded decision carries a non-empty target_folder. A `needs_review` outcome resolves target_folder to null (leave in place) and would 400 the required-target_folder receiver, so it is skipped here. Runs after the card so a move/relay failure never blocks the notification.

HTTP_ProcessingComplete.description: LCC relay (Option A caller): forward the AUTHORITATIVE recorded decision from HTTP_PostIntakeMessage's processing_complete block VERBATIM. /api/webhooks/processing-complete relays target_folder/disposition/clear_flag to pa-move-message.js, which fires the 'LCC Processing Complete → Move Message' flow. NEVER hardcode target_folder/disposition — the receiver relays the body verbatim and the pull-queue drain is retired, so a hardcoded value fires a REAL wrong move. staged → staging folder + KEEP flag; filed/duplicate → move + CLEAR flag. One POST per flagged email (single internet_message_id).
